data.body:
# Addendum: 5-requirement re-evaluation + OSS-validated pattern recommendation

User pinned 5 core requirements: (1) never review a huge diff in one review; (2) accumulate a huge feature off-main, land it all at once; (3) every change reaching main passed LLM review; (4) no invalidation fragility from content-comparison or merge/rebase instability; (5) pattern must be OSS-validated, not novel/Frankenstein. Note (4)+(5) eliminate the prior options: TRIVIAL_REBASE vote-carry IS content comparison; a custom narrowed interaction re-review IS a novel invention; current chain+topic fails (4) (rebases wipe votes).

## Recommendation: Gerrit server-side feature branches + reviewed merge change (the OpenDev-documented pattern)

Stories = small reviewed changes targeting refs/heads/feature/<name>, merged into the branch AS they pass (attestation consumed immediately into branch history — no vote ever needs to survive a rebase; branch history is the durable accumulator, not label state). Feature completion = one --no-ff merge commit of the branch into main, pushed to refs/for/main as its own reviewed "merge change", gated by the same submit requirement, submitted atomically. No rebases anywhere → requirement 4 by construction.

## OSS validation (web-research agent, 2026-07-01, URLs in agent report)
- OpenDev/OpenStack: Infra Manual documents the exact flow (server-side branch via UI; pushMerge ACL; git merge --no-ff + push refs/for/master). LIVE: openstack/swift feature/mpu + feature/threaded active 2025-2026 with story-sized reviewed changes + monthly reviewed master->feature catch-up merges; merge changes carry the standard label gate (change 983874: Verified+2/CR+2/Workflow+1).
- Qt: reviewed merge changes live on codereview.qt-project.org (qt-creator origin/20.0->master merges June 2026); caveat: Qt abandoned ROUTINE cross-branch merges for release maintenance in 2020 (QUIP-16) in favor of cherry-picks — different use case (long-lived release branches) than finite feature branches.
- Wikimedia: ad-hoc merge commits into master confirmed; not documented policy.
- Chromium/AOSP/Go: deliberately linear (chains/topics/CQ) — the alternative school; their objection (merges hide interaction cost, hurt bisect) is the pattern's known weakness, mitigated by CI on the merge change.

## Three corrections vs assumptions
1. Human UI default diff base for merge commits is FIRST_PARENT (whole feature diff), not Auto Merge — auto-merge (conflict-resolutions-only) is a dropdown selection; server default confirmed in Gerrit source (GeneralPreferencesInfo.java). REST default IS auto-merge.
2. BOT TRAP: GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch on a merge commit returns HTTP 409 ("Revision has more than 1 parent", GetPatch.java); patch?parent=1 returns the ENTIRE feature diff (violates req 1). No /patch variant gives vs-auto-merge. The bot must branch on parent count and use files/ (no parent param => auto-merge base) + per-file /diff (auto-merge base) + /mergelist. TODAY our voter would 409 -> fail-closed -1 on any merge change (safe failure, but blocks the pattern).
3. Gerrit has a purpose-built changekind MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE ("don't want to trigger CI or human verification again if your target branch moved forward", config-labels.html): add to LLM-Review copyCondition so re-merging after main advances doesn't wipe the vote; a new feature-tip is a REWORK and correctly re-reviews.

## Scorecard (feature-branch pattern): req1 PASS (story diffs small; merge change reviews conflict-delta vs auto-merge; caveat: textually-clean semantic conflicts show EMPTY auto-merge diff — CI on the merge change is the mitigation => open epic smoky-flare-slug/1fa8 Verified vote becomes a required complement, not optional). req2 PASS (feature branches live only in Gerrit; replication pushes main+tags only). req3 PASS (submit requirement has no branch clause — already gates feature branches; every commit reaching main was gated as story change or merge change). req4 PASS (no rebases; votes consumed at story-merge; MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE is Gerrit-native + doc-sanctioned). req5 PASS (OpenDev documented + live; honest caveats recorded).

## RED/BLUE/GREEN replay under the pattern
RED: change A -> feature branch, +1, submit into branch. BLUE: change B likewise (parallel worktrees fine — both target the branch; MERGE_IF_NECESSARY merges siblings without rebase). GREEN: pulls branch (A+B in history), builds C, small review, submit. Merge change to main: usually near-empty auto-merge delta + mergelist + CI. Zero re-review of A/B/C. Zero vote wipes.

## Concrete deltas to implement
a. infra/gerrit/project.config: copyCondition = "changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE OR changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE"; [submit] explicit mergeContent=true + pin submitType=MERGE_IF_NECESSARY (currently unset/inherited — was already flagged); ACL: Create Reference + Delete Reference on refs/heads/feature/*; Push Merge Commit on refs/for/refs/heads/main + refs/for/refs/heads/feature/*.
b. src/rebar/review_bot/: merge-change code path (detect via revisions/{rev}/commit parents length; assemble review input from files/ + per-file diff vs auto-merge + mergelist; keep fail-closed).
c. keep receive.rejectImplicitMerges compatible (it does NOT block explicit merge commits — config-project-config.html).
d. CONTRIBUTING.md + docs: feature-branch workflow (OpenDev recipe incl. Change-Id amend), branch-lifetime cap, scheduled catch-up merges, branch deletion after merge-back (no Gerrit auto-cleanup exists).
e. smoky-flare-slug (CI Verified vote): elevate to required for merge changes (auto-merge diff cannot catch semantic conflicts).
